Howdoes the issue of "okupas" in Catalonia

strengths: Overall structure follows a logical introductionâanalysisâconclusion flow
Language is generally understandable and conveys the main argument
weaknesses: Frequent typographical and grammatical errors hinder readability
Inconsistent formatting (placeholders, section numbering) and citation style reduce professionalism
Some paragraphs are cut off or poorly transitioned, affecting coherence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the report is organized enough to follow the argument (band 1â2), repeated errors in formatting, citations and language clarity limit communication to the lower end of the scale.

strengths: Issue clearly stated with precise contextual background on Cataloniaâs okupas situation
Strong linkage of the projectâs importance to human rights, legitimacy and justice concepts
Engagement activities are wellâmatched to the issue and their relevance is convincingly explained
weaknesses: Introductory claims lack source attribution and citations for statistics
Justification of why the IA format is uniquely suitable could be developed further
Some theoretical applications (e.g. Rawls) need explicit citation to bolster academic rigor
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission excels in identifying and explaining the political issue with depth and relevance (band 3â4), and convincingly justifies why the topic matters. Engagement activities are linked clearly. Minor gaps in source attribution and explicit reference to the IA format keep it below a perfect 4.

strengths: Purposeful selection of diverse stakeholders demonstrates intentional planning
Use of primary legal texts shows commitment to substantive research
weaknesses: No structured timeline or detailed methodology is provided
Logistical planning for engagements is thin and lacks contingency measures
Integration between research findings and engagement is intermittent rather than systematic
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While there is evidence of adâhoc planning and thoughtful stakeholder choice (band 1â2), the lack of a coherent timeline, sparse logistical detail and only occasional feedback loops limit this criterion to the lower end of the scale.

strengths: Purposeful application of course concepts (e.g. Rawlsâ justice, Lockeâs social contract)
Analysis moves beyond description to weigh trade-offs across economic, legal and moral dimensions
Stakeholder perspectives are presented and contrasted to support the argument
weaknesses: Analysis remains at a surface level without fully multi-layered, nuanced exploration
Synthesis of differing viewpoints is partial; more coherent weaving of perspectives is needed
Claims about organised crime or market impacts lack empirical substantiation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work achieves an adequate, mostly accurate use of concepts and provides balanced analysis (band 3â4), but does not reach the depth or integration for higher marks. Thus a mid-range score of 2 is appropriate.

strengths: The student acknowledges initial bias and notes how engagement broadened their perspective
Some evaluation of source gaps and the superiority of interview insights over secondary research
weaknesses: Methodological limitations and reliability issues are barely explored
Reflection on personal bias does not examine its impact on research design or interpretation
Future implications and concrete follow-up actions are not articulated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present but largely superficial (band 1â2), with minimal critical evaluation of methods or deep insight into how bias shaped the work. This merits a low score in line with the strand assessments.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The student explicitly states the political issueâ the clash between the right to housing and the right to property in the context of âokupasâ in Cataloniaâ in the title and first paragraph.  
â¢ A succinct background (statistics on occupations, legal changes since 2008, geographical focus on Catalonia) clarifies scale and location.  
â¢ Explanations link the issue to key Global Politics concepts (human rights, legitimacy, justice) and outline why it is contentious. This gives precise, relevant context rather than a superficial overview. Hence the top band is merited and the higher score within that band is justified.

Evidence of planning exists: secondary reading preceded stakeholder outreach; legal texts were identified before interviews; confidence-building for question design is mentioned.  
Yet no structured timeline, source criteria or systematic methodology is provided, so the work exceeds ad-hoc level but does not reach the âwell-structuredâ descriptor.

Stakeholders were purposefully selected, and attempts to contact the mayor are noted, showing some foresight.  
Nevertheless, details on scheduling, logistics, or risk-management are thin, so the engagement plan is present but patchy, aligning with the 2-mark descriptor.

Concepts such as UDHR rights, Beethamâs legitimacy, Rawlsâ justice, Lockeâs social contract and proviso are all introduced and applied to concrete aspects of the case.  
While largely accurate, some uses remain basic (e.g., legitimacy claim stated but not fully unpacked), so the work fits the âadequate, mostly accurateâ bracket.

Initial desk research informed interview question development and stakeholder choice; interview data are later used in analysis.  
However, there is limited evidence of iterative feedback loops (e.g., new research arising from engagement findings), so links are intermittent rather than consistent.

Three interviews (housing-rights NGO, victimsâ platform founder, broker) are explained, with rationale for selecting each stakeholder and brief summaries of insights gained.  
Links between each engagement and the central clash of rights are explicitly drawn (e.g., broker on market impact, PAH on Rawlsian justice). The connection is convincing though not deeply evaluated, warranting the higher band but mid-level score.

Language is generally clear and arguments understandable, yet frequent grammatical slips (e.g., âinguiokupas,â âhereforeâ), run-on sentences and formatting errors hinder full coherence.  
These occasional lapses place the work above the minimum but below the consistently clear level.

The student moves beyond descriptionâ weighing government trade-offs, market impacts, and moral claimsâ and references multiple structural factors (post-2008 crisis, COVID-19 decree).  
Yet the analysis is not multi-layered or deeply comparative; economic, social and political dimensions are touched but not exhaustively probed, so the lower end of the 3-4 band is appropriate.

The narrative acknowledges an initial bias (âpreviously only one sideâ) and indicates how engagement broadened understanding.  
Still, the impact of bias on research choices or interpretations is not deeply examined. This meets âsome biases identifiedâ but falls short of an in-depth exploration.

The report follows a logical Introduction-Analysis-Conclusion format, but abrupt section breaks ({0}â¦{8}) and some repetition interrupt flow.  
Organisation is therefore basic and sometimes confusing, aligning with the 2-mark band.

The student notes data gaps and mentions that interview insights surpassed secondary sources, but critique of methodology, source reliability or interview limitations is minimal.  
Judgements remain surface-level; thus the strand earns marks in the 1â2 band at the upper end.

The write-up clearly argues that the investigation matters because it affects thousands of owners and vulnerable families, shapes government legitimacy and touches multiple human-rights obligations. Personal motivation (auntâs house, media salience) and academic relevance (Global Politics themes) are both articulated.  
However, the justification is occasionally descriptive and could link more directly to how the IA format is uniquely suitable. This places the work solidly in the top band but at the lower score.

Perspectives from homeowners, brokers, activists and policymakers are presented and occasionally contrasted (e.g., PAH vs. Bravo).  
Some connective argument is made about shared beliefs in needed reform, but overall weaving of viewpoints into a single coherent argument is partial rather than fully integrated.

Reflection explains how the engagement enhanced the studentâs ability to analyse future news and shifted personal views.  
Insights are meaningful but not thoroughly linked to future academic or civic action, corresponding to adequate (not in-depth) reflective learning.

annotations.comment: The title and issue statement clearly delineate the clash between housing rights and property rights in Catalonia, offering immediate clarity and contextual framing for the reader.
The label â1994â and missing word count placeholder create confusion; consider removing extraneous placeholders and ensuring consistent headings for clarity.
The use of quantitative data on squatters provides a solid empirical foundation, but consider citing the data source to enhance credibility.
This sentence is cut off mid-thought; revise paragraph continuity to ensure complete statements and smoother transitions.
The introductory statement lacks source attribution; include references to the original news reports to substantiate claims.
Describing the homeownerâs prolonged financial and legal burden effectively illustrates the human dimension of policy trade-offs.
Application of Rawlsâ theory of justice as fairness deepens theoretical analysis and shows purposeful use of global politics concepts.
Include a citation for Rawlsâ concept of Redistributive Justice to substantiate theoretical application.
The personal anecdote about the studentâs aunt enhances engagement and demonstrates genuine relevance to the research topic.
Invoking primary legal documents shows purposeful research planning and a commitment to understanding the legal framework.
Consider including a detailed timeline or Gantt chart to map research stages and stakeholder outreach for clearer demonstration of planning.
Selection of a diverse range of stakeholders demonstrates thoughtful engagement planning and aligns interviewees to different facets of the issue.
Interview summaries detail content but omit discussion of methodological limitations such as potential interviewee bias or validity of responses.
Integrating stakeholder insight on market impacts enriches the analysis by linking economic factors directly to policy outcomes.
There is a missing closing quotation mark in the Beetham citation and some typographical errors; proofread to maintain professional clarity.
Provide empirical evidence or case examples to substantiate claims about organised crime groups exploiting squatting laws.
Reflection on personal bias acknowledges shift but lacks exploration of how bias shaped research questions or interpretation.
The conclusion succinctly captures the complexity of balancing human rights and property by summarizing key dilemmas.
To strengthen reflective insight, specify concrete future actions or civic engagements you plan following this research.
Bibliography lacks a consistent citation format; apply a recognized style guide and maintain uniformity across entries.
Include access dates and use italics for publication titles to enhance bibliographic completeness and professionalism.
Listing AI tools without evaluating their credibility undermines source reliability; reflect on the vetting process for these modern tools.
Critically reflect on whether Rawlsâ theory fully accommodates complex real-world power imbalances in the okupas context.
Integration of Lockeâs social-contract perspective adds normative depth and connects theoretical concepts to real policy dilemmas.
Ensure the conclusion heading aligns consistently with earlier section numbering and style for structural cohesion.
Consider elaborating on how identified data gaps specifically impacted your analytical conclusions.
Describe the specific insights gained from ChatGPT and Perplexity and critically assess their reliability compared to peer-reviewed sources.

rubricData.name: Global Politics IA: Engagement Activity
rubricData.description: For the new Global Politics syllabus

strands.name: Political issue identification & clarity
strands.content:
1. Issue not identified or irrelevant
2. No meaningful explanation

strands.name: Project importance & suitability
strands.content: No discussion of why the project matters

strands.name: Relevance of engagement activities
strands.content: Activities absent or unrelated to issue

strands.name: Political issue identification & clarity
strands.content:
1. Issue named but explanation vague / superficial
2. Limited or missing context

strands.name: Project importance & suitability
strands.content: Importance / suitability mentioned but weak or generic

strands.name: Relevance of engagement activities
strands.content: Activities listed; relevance implied or weakly linked

strands.name: Political issue identification & clarity
strands.content:
1. Issue explicitly stated
2. Clear, precise explanation with relevant context

strands.name: Project importance & suitability
strands.content: Importance / suitability clearly justified with specific reasons

strands.name: Relevance of engagement activities
strands.content: Activities fully explained and convincingly linked to the issue

strands.name: Integration of research â engagement
strands.content: No link between research and engagement

strands.name: Engagement planning
strands.content: Activities planned but timing / logistics patchy

strands.name: Integration of research â engagement
strands.content: Research consistently informs engagement; findings feed back into further inquiry

strands.name: Depth of political-issue analysis
strands.content: Clear, balanced analysis; key factors explored

strands.name: Synthesis of stakeholder perspectives
strands.content: Effective synthesis; perspectives woven into coherent argument

strands.name: Awareness of personal bias
strands.content: Biases explored; impact on work examined

strands.name: Organization & structure
strands.content: Logical structure; mostly easy to follow
